ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific term for xiao long bao; 'dumpling food product' (FOODON_03315091) is the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for xiao long bao; A0FKP (Dumplings, steamed) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for xiao long bao or steamed soup dumplings.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of steamed pork xiao long bao consists of 6 pieces, weighing approximately 180 g in total, as typically served at Din Tai Fung and comparable Shanghainese restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Xiao Long Bao (Steamed Pork Dumpling) — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo lists steamed pork dumplings (xiao long bao) with nutrient values per 100 g. Values are adapted from the HPB Foodo database entry for steamed pork dumplings. Serving size of 180 g (6 pieces at approximately 30 g each) is consistent with Din Tai Fung standard portion. Where HPB Foodo does not report a micronutrient, the value is set to -1 per sentinel convention.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — steamed wheat dumplings / dim sum analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for xiao long bao in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 48 is estimated by analogy with steamed wheat-based dim sum items (e.g., steamed pork buns, har gow wrappers) which cluster in the GI range 45–55 in peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2021, Diabetes Care). The wheat-flour wrapper is the primary glycemic driver; pork filling and gelatin broth contribute negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 42.3) / 100 ≈ 20.3, where carbohydrate per 180 g serving = 23.5 × 1.8 = 42.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (420 mg per 100 g; approximately 756 mg per 6-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit xiao long bao intake to occasional consumption, as a single 6-piece serving provides approximately 756 mg sodium — about one-third of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — and should avoid adding soy sauce or chilli dipping condiments which further increase sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ≈ 20 per 6-piece serving) driven by refined wheat flour wrapper
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume xiao long bao in moderated portions (3–4 pieces rather than 6) as the low-GI wheat wrapper and protein-rich pork filling attenuate postprandial glucose response, but the total glycemic load of a full 6-piece serving is moderate and should be accounted for within the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (2.9 g per 100 g; approximately 5.2 g per serving) and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should consume xiao long bao in moderation, as each 6-piece serving contributes approximately 5.2 g saturated fat and 756 mg sodium; pairing with unsalted ginger and vinegar rather than soy-based dips and limiting frequency to once weekly is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Pork Xiao Long Bao (小笼包) are traditional Shanghainese soup dumplings consisting of a thin, delicate wheat-flour wrapper encasing a seasoned minced pork filling and a small pocket of rich pork gelatin broth. During steaming, the gelatin melts into a hot, flavourful soup that bursts in the mouth upon biting. The dough is hand-pleated with a minimum of 18 folds at the top, a hallmark of skilled craftsmanship. Xiao long bao are served in bamboo steamers and traditionally eaten with julienned ginger and Chinkiang black vinegar. In Singapore, Din Tai Fung is the most prominent purveyor, serving a 6-piece portion. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 210 kcal, 10.5 g protein, 8.2 g fat, and 23.5 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is notable at 420 mg per 100 g, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low), driven primarily by the refined wheat wrapper, though the protein and fat content of the filling moderate postprandial glucose response. Saturated fat is moderate at 2.9 g per 100 g. Xiao long bao are not suitable for halal or vegetarian diets in their standard pork form. They contain gluten and soy allergens. The dish is a staple of Shanghainese dim sum culture and is widely consumed across Singapore, Taiwan, and the Chinese diaspora globally.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo steamed pork dumpling entries, which represent the closest approved primary source for this Singaporean-served Chinese dish. GI is an analogous estimate based on comparable steamed wheat dim sum items from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2021). No exact xiao long bao entry with full micronutrient panel was found in a single approved source; therefore confidence is medium rather than high.
notes: Serving size of 180 g is based on 6 pieces at approximately 30 g each, consistent with Din Tai Fung standard portion weight. Nutrient values per 100 g are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo steamed pork dumpling data. Micronutrients vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1 as they are not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry for this food category. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate; no direct xiao long bao GI measurement was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per serving = (48 × 42.3) / 100 ≈ 20.3 g, where carbohydrate per 180 g serving = 42.3 g. The dish contains pork and is not halal or vegetarian. Allergens include gluten (wheat wrapper) and soy (soy sauce in filling). Din Tai Fung's version is noted for its minimum 18-fold pleating technique. Price range SGD 11.99–16.35 per 6-piece serving as listed on field survey data.
